statement.en: On a Common Boundary Geometry for Cosmic Expansion and Quantum State Reduction
statement.summary: Two unresolved problems remain central in modern physics: cosmic acceleration at the macroscopic scale and objective wave function collapse at the microscopic scale. Both are usually treated in separate frameworks and at the level of three-dimensional bulk dynamics. A different possibility is considered here. The same two problems are treated in terms of two-dimensional boundary geometry through a scale-invariant master equation. Within this construction, cosmic expansion and quantum state reduction are represented as distinct regimes of a common boundary-based framework. Information transfer is written as a boundary-crossing process governed by geometric scale, transition frequency, and a dimensionless transmission efficiency. The macroscopic limit is associated with horizon-scale boundar

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom proposes a novel unifying framework (boundary geometry coupling cosmic expansion and quantum collapse) that is mathematically speculative, lacks empirical validation, and remains in preprint status (2026 timestamp indicates provisional work). The core claims are neither established theorems nor refuted, but represent a working hypothesis under development. Classification as FLOWING reflects the provisional, non-validated character of the theoretical construction.
